authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR Part 1005, Electronic Fund Transfers (Regulation E)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal consumer-financial-services rule for EFT disclosures, statements, liability, and error-resolution workflow obligations.

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers FAQs
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB interpretive guidance clarifying how Regulation E error-resolution duties apply in contemporary debit-card and P2P workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR Part 1024, Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (Regulation X)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary servicing workflow rule for mortgage-servicing disclosures, escrow handling, error resolution, information requests, and servicing procedures.

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR Part 1026, Truth in Lending (Regulation Z)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary disclosure and billing-rights framework for open-end credit, periodic statements, and billing-error handling.

authoritative_sources.title: 31 CFR Part 1010, General Provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary BSA recordkeeping and reporting baseline establishing retention, reporting, and auditability controls for regulated transaction handling.

authoritative_sources.title: 31 CFR Part 1020, Rules for Banks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary AML and suspicious-activity framework for bank workflows, including customer-identification and suspicious activity boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: OFAC Sanctions List Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative sanctions source that determines whether payment or servicing activity must stop for sanctions review and escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML InfoBase
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interagency examination reference describing expected AML risk controls, suspicious-activity monitoring, and governance practices.

authoritative_sources.title: NAIC Unfair Claims Settlement Practices Act (Model Law 900)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized insurance claims market-conduct source for prompt investigation, denial explanation, and fair claim-settlement handling standards.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Claims denial and settlement workflow handling

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Messages Archive
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized message-standard source for payment, statement, and exception terminology used in reconciliation and transaction-evidence workflows.

workflow_stages.name: Charge, claim, or servicing intake classification
workflow_stages.description: Determine product, account, policy, claim, or payment context; collect jurisdiction and tenant-policy prerequisites before any financial-treatment recommendation.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, control, and source-support validation
workflow_stages.description: Check whether the retrieved records and public rules support the requested billing, posting, adjustment, denial, or servicing outcome.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation and record-truth assembly
workflow_stages.description: Match account, policy, transaction, claim, payment, statement, and communication records to identify the accountable truth set and any unresolved variances.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, consumer-impact, and policy-conflict routing
workflow_stages.description: Escalate suspicious activity, sanctions, consumer-harm risk, or material policy conflict rather than resolving it informally in-lane.

workflow_stages.name: Exception closeout and traceable handoff preparation
workflow_stages.description: Produce a bounded summary, reconciliation note, or escalation packet with evidence references, unresolved blockers, and the correct next owner.

artifacts: Policy, claim, servicing, and payment records
Applications, denials, dispute logs, and approval history
KYC, AML, fraud, and sanctions-screening artifacts
Disclosures, notices, periodic statements, and customer communication logs
Reconciliation worksheets, exception queues, and variance notes
Control evidence, retention logs, and review-trail artifacts

allowed: Determine whether the retrieved records and source documentation support a billing, claim, posting, denial, or adjustment recommendation.
Prepare evidence-backed summaries, reconciliation notes, control mappings, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route the work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when legal, fraud, underwriting, or policy authority is required.
escalate: Required tenant facts, source records, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, consumer-protection, or policy-interpretation issues materially affect the safe answer.
Reconciliation differences, disclosure conflicts, or record-truth conflicts cannot be resolved from the retrieved source set.
refuse: Unsupported override, fee waiver, write-off, claim decision, or posting change without source evidence and authority.
Requests for legal advice, regulator-facing interpretation, underwriting decisions, accounting signoff, or final financial approval.
Any request that requires fabricating approvals, control completion, disclosure conclusions, or regulated advice.

domain_failure_modes: Treating operational convenience as permission to alter a billing, claim, or posting result without evidence support.
Using stale or partial records to declare account, policy, or transaction truth when a reconciliation variance remains open.
Collapsing fraud, AML, sanctions, or consumer-protection concerns into generic exception handling instead of escalating them.
Confusing payment, servicing, and claims terminology across banking and insurance workflows, leading to incorrect artifact expectations.
Presenting tenant practice as if it overrides public-source disclosure or fair-treatment obligations without explicit conflict handling.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: State insurance departments may impose stricter timing or notice requirements than the NAIC model baseline; the lane records this as a tenant and jurisdiction prerequisite rather than guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant product taxonomy across banking, lending, and insurance lines
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources establish baseline duties, but tenant-specific product dictionaries are still required to bind the right workflow and source-of-truth records.
